mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate signals stronger labor market conditions, boosting corporate earnings and consumer spending, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE declines by >0.5pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong consumer and business demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through increased corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of instances where RSAFS and HOUST both rise by >1% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening supply-side capacity, which increases inflationary pressures and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days due to central bank intervention.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO declines by >1.5pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in CPI inflation above 2% signals central bank hawkishness, leading to higher real interest rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions, which supports consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS rises by >100k month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) during late-cycle expansions signals overheating demand, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days due to policy tightening risks.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where GDPC1 and HOUST both rise by >1% quarter-over-quarter.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) signal a tightening cycle, which raises discount rates and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL both rise by >0.5pp month-over-month.
